prose:
Method–provenance block (reproducibility summary)

• Property: HOMO–LUMO (frontier-orbital) gap of ethylene, in eV  
• Level of theory: density-fitted DFT, tier ‘standard’ = B3LYP/def2-TZVP (RI-JK)  
• Geometry provenance: fixed input geometry taken from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/ethylene/ethylene.xyz; no further optimisation (single-point evaluation, i.e. DFT//input-xyz)  
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvation)  
• Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet)  
• Software/backend: PySCF (invoked through chemkit frontier-orbitals skill)  
• SCF convergence: converged in 6 cycles; gradient threshold satisfied  
• Warnings: no warnings reported by the engine  
• Output artefacts: result JSON written to  
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp8bgvy2ct/live.json  
  (the live engine log was streamed to the default frontier_<timestamp>.out file in the caller’s working directory during the run)

Key results (gas-phase B3LYP/def2-TZVP):
• Total electronic energy  E = –78.623486 Ha (–2139.4541 eV)  
• HOMO energy             ε_H = –7.6155818 eV  
• LUMO energy             ε_L = –0.17986527 eV  
• HOMO–LUMO gap           Δε  = 7.4357165 eV   ← headline value

The calculation was carried out with chemkit’s “frontier-orbitals” skill, requesting `--method dft --tier standard` (which expands to B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI-JK density fitting). The result above is therefore entirely computed; no experimental comparison is offered here.
